reasoning: As of late May 2025, Beto O'Rourke has transitioned into a highly active 'testing the waters' phase for the 2026 Texas Senate race. Throughout April and May 2025, he has held numerous rallies and town halls across Texas (Denton, Amarillo, Dallas, Garland) and explicitly stated that he 'will' run if Texans want him to. Historically, O'Rourke announced his 2018 Senate bid in March of the preceding year, suggesting a preference for early entry in Senate contests to allow for the massive fundraising required in Texas. Announcing before July 1, 2025, would allow him to capitalize on his current tour's momentum and report his first round of fundraising for the Q2 (June 30) deadline. While his 2022 gubernatorial run saw a longer gap between signaling (May) and formal announcement (November), the current intensity of his rally circuit (5+ events in May 2025) and the early entry of Republican heavyweight Ken Paxton suggest a more accelerated timeline. The 40-day window remaining until July 1 makes a formal declaration very likely, though not certain given his past periods of deliberation.
